overview.short: Sajjangad, meaning "Fort of Good People", is located near the city of Satara, India.
overview.description: Sajjangad, meaning "Fort of Good People", is located near the city of Satara, India. It is the final resting place of Sant Ramdas in 18th century India (born 1608). His teachings and works written in books such as Dasbodh are read and followed by many people even today in the state of Maharashtra and Sajjangad is a popular place of pilgrimage.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Thoseghar Waterfalls
topPlaces.description: Thoseghar waterfalls is a scenic spot located near the small village of Thoseghar, 20 km from Satara city, at the edge of the Konkan region, in Western India. There are a series of waterfalls, some of them 15 to 20 metres and one of approx 200 metres in height.

topPlaces.name: Ajinkyatara
topPlaces.description: Ajinkyatara Fort, also known as the "Fort of the Sapta-Rishi," is a historical hill fort located on Ajinkyatara Mountain, one of the seven mountains surrounding the city of Satara in the Sahyadri Mountains of Maharashtra, India. Situated at an altitude of 3,300 feet, the fort overlooks Satara City and is considered one of the major heritage sites in Maharashtra.

topPlaces.name: Kanher Dam
topPlaces.description: Kanher Dam, is an earthfill and gravity dam on Wenna river near Satara in state of Maharashtra in India.

topPlaces.name: Vele, Satara
topPlaces.description: Vele is a village in the Western region of Maharashtra state in India. It is located in the Satara district of Maharashtra.

items.note: Thoseghar waterfalls is a scenic spot located near the small village of Thoseghar, 20 km from Satara city, at the edge of the Konkan region, in Western India. There are a series of waterfalls, some of them 15 to 20 metres and one of approx 200 metres in height.

Ajinkyatara Fort, also known as the "Fort of the Sapta-Rishi," is a historical hill fort located on Ajinkyatara Mountain, one of the seven mountains surrounding the city of Satara in the Sahyadri Mountains of Maharashtra, India. Situated at an altitude of 3,300 feet, the fort overlooks Satara City and is considered one of the major heritage sites in Maharashtra.
Kanher Dam, is an earthfill and gravity dam on Wenna river near Satara in state of Maharashtra in India.
Vele is a village in the Western region of Maharashtra state in India. It is located in the Satara district of Maharashtra.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Sajjangad?
faq.a: The best time to visit Sajjangad is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

The nearest airport is Pune Airport (104 km) and the nearest railway station is Pune Junction (98 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Sajjangad?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Sajjangad at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Sajjangad?
faq.a: Stays in Sajjangad start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Sajjangad?
faq.a: The top attractions are Thoseghar Waterfalls, Ajinkyatara, Kanher Dam, Vele, Satara.
